debug_info.goal:
p : ℕ
inst✝¹ : Fact (Nat.Prime p)
inst✝ : NeZero p
el : ZMod p
er : Exp (ZMod p) (ZMod p)
cl : Option Unit
cr : Circuit p (ZMod p)
w : ℕ
he : el = er.eval
hc : Simulation.s_bisim cl cr.eval
x✝¹ : Option Unit
x✝ : Unit → Option Unit
her : er.eval.val < 2 ^ w
heq : 2 ^ w ≤ er.eval.val
⊢ Simulation.s_bisim none cr.eval

failed_attempts:
simp at h
    rw [he] at h
    simp
    split
    . apply hc
    . contradiction

simp at h
    rw [he] at h
    simp at h
    contradiction

· -- Subcase where the condition is false (el.val >= 2^w)
      simp
      <;> contradiction

simp at *
    <;> tauto

all_goals try tauto
  case _ _ default her =>
    simp at her
    simp [he, assert_range] at *
    all_goals try tauto
  case _ _ default default =>
    simp at *
    all_goals try tauto
h

-- `her` is `er.eval.val < 2 ^ w`
    -- `heq` is `2 ^ w ≤ er.eval.val`
    -- These lead to `2 ^ w ≤ er.eval.val < 2 ^ w`, which implies `2 ^ w < 2 ^ w`, a contradiction.
    exfalso
    linarith -- linarith can find the contradiction from `her` and `heq`
  case _ _ _ _ _ => -- This case is unreachable due to the structure of the `split` and `simp` above
    simp at *
    all_goals try tauto

all_goals try exact hc
  · simp [h]
    all_goals try exact hc

          omega 
        have h2 : er.eval.val < 2 ^ w := by 
          omega 
        omega 
      }
      <;> try { 
        have h1 : 2 ^ w ≤ er.eval.val := by 
          omega 
        have h2 : er.eval.val < 2 ^ w := by 
          omega 
        omega 
      }
    }

omit [NeZero p] in
lemma equiv_share (el : ZMod p) (er : Exp (ZMod p) (ZMod p)) (kl : ZMod p -> Option Unit) (kr : ZMod p -> Circuit p (ZMod p)) :
  el = Exp.eval er ->
  (∀ x, Simulation.s_bisim (kl x) (Circuit.eval (kr x))) ->
  Simulation.s_bisim (bind (share el) kl) (Circuit.eval (.share er kr)) := by
  intro he hk
  simp only [Circuit.eval,bind,share]
  rw [he]
  apply hk

lemma equiv_assert_range (el : ZMod p) (er : Exp (ZMod p) (ZMod p)) (cl : Option Unit) (cr : Circuit p (ZMod p)) (w : ℕ) :
  el = Exp.eval er ->
  Simulation.s_bisim cl (Circuit.eval cr) ->
  Simulation.s_bisim (Option.bind (assert_range w el) (fun () => cl)) (Circuit.eval (.assert_range w er cr)) := by
  intro he hc
  simp only [Circuit.eval,Option.bind,assert_range]
  split
  split
  case _ _ heq her =>
    simp at heq
    rw [he] at heq
--    contradiction
    sorry

simp at h
    rw [he] at h
    simp at h
    contradiction

simp at her
    apply hc

linarith
  case _ _ hne hle =>
    simp at hle hne
    rw [he] at hne hle
    exfalso
    linarith
